theme: factory arms sharing one breaker during a blackout drill
title: Breaker Drill: Water-Filling K Robot Arms Under One Power Cap
strategies: serialize every arm at speed 1, one distance unit per tick (trivial)
run each arm alone at its solo max feasible speed floor(sqrt(P)), one arm at a time in input order (greedy: wastes the cap on non-critical arms by never overlapping them with the critical arm)

water-filling: pick a shared makespan T balancing the A*T vs sum(D_i^2)/T trade-off, then run ALL arms in parallel at a near-constant per-arm speed D_i/T (equalizing marginal delay per watt across arms) so slack arms spend far less energy while the critical arm still finishes at essentially T (strong)
fully adaptive per-tick dual/multiplier retuning across the whole schedule, jointly re-optimizing T and the tick-by-tick allocation (open ceiling, uncovered)
